text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavor_text.en: Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.
Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard a Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you can't discard a Plasma Energy, this attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: For some reason, it likes to land on people's heads softly and act like it's a hat.

text.en: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavor_text.en: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

text.en: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavor_text.en: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavor_text.en: It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock.

text.en: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

text.en: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

rules_text.en: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

rules_text.en: Put a Pokémon from your hand face down in front of you and tell your opponent its name. Your opponent guesses the height of that Pokémon. Reveal that Pokémon. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 3 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 3 cards. Return the Pokémon to your hand. (You can’t choose a Pokémon that doesn’t have the height printed on the card.)
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

text.en: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavor_text.en: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.
When it finds a stone of a suitable size, it secretes a liquid from its mouth to open up a hole to crawl into.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: When its boulder is broken in battles for territory, it feels unsure and begins to weaken.

text.en: Draw 2 cards.

flavor_text.en: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

text.en: Does 20 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

text.en: Attach 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

text.en: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: In the rocky mountains where Charmeleon live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

flavor_text.en: It is said that Charizard's fire burns hotter if it has experienced harsh battles.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavor_text.en: Even if one of its arms is eaten, it's fine. The Pokémon regenerates quickly and will go right back to normal.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavor_text.en: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

text.en: If this Pokémon has any basic Energy attached to it that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavor_text.en: Each of its nine tails is imbued with supernatural power, and it can live for a thousand years.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavor_text.en: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

flavor_text.en: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

flavor_text.en: It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

text.en: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When it trouble, it emits smoke.
It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

flavor_text.en: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

flavor_text.en: A Magikarp living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.

text.en: Does 20 damage times the number of Prize cards both players have taken.

flavor_text.en: Once it begins to rampage, a Gyarados will burn everything down, even in a harsh storm.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavor_text.en: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavor_text.en: Because every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.

text.en: If this Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavor_text.en: If anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.

Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavor_text.en: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavor_text.en: The scalchop on its stomach is made from the same elements as claws. It detaches the scalchop for use as a blade.

text.en: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 10 more damage for each heads.

flavor_text.en: Scalchop techniques differ from one Dewott to another. It never neglects maintaining its scalchops.

text.en: Does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: Part of the armor on its anterior legs becomes a giant sword. Its cry alone is enough to intimidate most enemies.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavor_text.en: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.
By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

text.en: Does 20 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.

flavor_text.en: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to capture prey.

text.en: Does 30 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.

flavor_text.en: It increases the power of its punches by vibrating the bumps on its fists. It can turn a boulder to rubble with one punch.

flavor_text.en: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

flavor_text.en: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

flavor_text.en: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

flavor_text.en: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

flavor_text.en: Using its tentacles to lash itself to trees, it lies in wait for prey, luring it close with sweet-smelling drool.

flavor_text.en: It picks food from cactus plants, deftly avoiding buds and spines. It seems to skip about to move.

flavor_text.en: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun's movement all day.
A Pokémon that startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

flavor_text.en: Its cry sounds like an incantation. It is said the cry may rarely be imbued with happiness-giving power.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavor_text.en: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

flavor_text.en: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavor_text.en: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

flavor_text.en: The croaking that Toxicroak produces before a battle is for churning the poison it has stored in its poison sac.

flavor_text.en: The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a Woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for 2 Psychic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavor_text.en: It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble.

flavor_text.en: The guardians of an ancient city, they use their psychic power to attack enemies that invade their territory.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.
It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

flavor_text.en: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

flavor_text.en: They avoid attacks by sinking into the shadows of thick foliage. They retaliate with masterful whipping techniques.

flavor_text.en: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

flavor_text.en: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.
They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others

text.en: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they are fine in any environment, no matter how severe.
Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.
